mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in > 60% of triggered instances or signal loses predictive power when unemployment is below 4.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate broad-based domestic demand expansion, which boosts corporate earnings expectations and produces positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when either series shows month-over-month decline, or interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production index values reflect strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which translates to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon during economic expansion phases.
failure_condition: Signal direction reverses or becomes noise during recessionary periods, or 5-day forward returns show no significant correlation (Spearman rho < 0.05) with INDPRO changes.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month CPI increase exceeding 0.4 percent triggers expectations of aggressive monetary tightening and margin compression, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Market prices in inflation rapidly and forward returns turn positive, or the signal loses predictive accuracy when CPI growth is driven by volatile energy components.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a decline in the unemployment rate confirm robust labor market tightening, supporting higher consumer income and generating positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The combined signal produces negative forward returns in > 50% of occurrences, or the interaction effect vanishes when payroll growth falls below 100,000.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real gross domestic product reflects expanding economic activity and corporate revenue fundamentals, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: GDP revisions consistently downgrade initial estimates and forward returns turn negative, or the signal shows zero predictive power (Rank IC = 0) across multiple quarters.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the industrial production index exceeding 0.5 percent signals a sharp contraction in manufacturing activity and supply chain stress, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in > 65% of triggered instances, or the threshold fails to separate winning and losing periods with statistical significance.
